commit.message:
fix(phrases): 修正 GamepadMessageBox 出現時 PhraseManagerDialog 仍可手把操作的問題

DeleteSelectedPhrase 呼叫 GamepadMessageBox.Show 時，未解除
PhraseManagerDialog 自身的控制器事件訂閱，導致兩個對話框
同時接收相同的手把按鈕事件（APressed、BPressed 等），造成：
- 確認對話框按 B 同時觸發 GamepadMessageBox 取消與
  PhraseManagerDialog.HandleClose，關閉整個對話框
- 確認對話框按 A 同時觸發 GamepadMessageBox 確認與
  PhraseManagerDialog.HandleGamepadA，誤觸插入或新增流程

修正：
1. DeleteSelectedPhrase 改為先呼叫 UnsubscribeGamepadEvents()，
   以 try/finally 包住 GamepadMessageBox.Show，離開後重新
   SubscribeGamepadEvents() 並視需要 Resume 控制器，
   與 AddPhrase / EditSelectedPhrase 保持一致。
2. 所有八個 Handler 在 SafeInvoke lambda 最前端加入
   ActiveForm != this 守衛，防止背景輪詢執行緒的 in-flight
   事件在子對話框開啟期間穿透至本對話框。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct HSC/D00/LDS playback speed — derive BPM from refresh rate

adplug_get_bpm_value() returned hardcoded 125 for formats without a native
BPM concept (HSC, D00, LDS, etc.). The TS extractor trusted this value,
producing 25 rows/sec instead of the correct 9.1 for HSC (18.2Hz / speed 2).

Now returns 0 for non-MOD/S3M formats, causing the extractor to derive BPM
from the player's refresh rate: BPM = refresh * 5 / 2 = 46 for HSC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct word highlight color based on accuracy score only

Add FL compliance config models, controller, and seed data (#634)

* Add FL compliance config models, controller, and seed data

Introduce TenantComplianceConfig (Cosmos DB document) and StateComplianceConfig
(embedded value object) with FL AHCA / SMMC 3.0 regulatory defaults: prompt pay
deadlines, PA timelines, appeal windows, encounter submission limits, FMMIS
submitter credentials, and MPIP flag. Add ComplianceConfigController for runtime
lookups and a seed JSON for Florida defaults.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Refine compliance config controller routes, add auth and Cosmos registration

Rewrite ComplianceConfigController to match API spec:
  GET  /api/compliance-config/{tenantId}       -> full TenantComplianceConfig
  GET  /api/compliance-config/{tenantId}/state -> StateComplianceConfig only
  PUT  /api/compliance-config/{tenantId}       -> upsert (AdminPolicy required)

Add Cosmos DB client registration and AdminPolicy authorization to Program.cs.
Enrich fl-ahca-config.json seed with regulatory comments citing FL Statute
627.6131, SMMC contract sections, and AHCA encounter requirements.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Add FMMIS Companion Guide and claim transformer for FL 837 encounters

Implement FL-specific EDI layer in claims-service/EDI/Florida/:

- FmmisCompanionGuide.cs: static constants for FMMIS ISA/GS headers,
  REF*1D qualifier, SBR/BHT codes, acknowledgment enums (TA1/997/999),
  and ValidateFmmisCompliance() pre-flight check (LOB, adjudication
  status, 60-day window, required fields).

- FmmisClaimTransformer.cs: transforms adjudicated Claim to FMMIS-
  compliant 837P/837I applying four Companion Guide rules:
  1) Subscriber = member (no 2000C dependent loop, NM109 = Medicaid ID)
  2) REF*1D in 2010AA with FL Medicaid Provider Number via IProviderService
  3) ISA08 = 'FMMIS' receiver ID
  4) BHT02 = '18' encounter purpose code
  Pure BuildEdi() for unit testing; async Transform() with DI.

- Models/FmmisSubmissionResult.cs: FmmisTransaction output DTO and
  FmmisSubmissionResult batch wrapper.

- Models/FmmisValidationException.cs: exception carrying all validation
  errors for single-pass reporting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Add FMMIS file builder, update models, register DI services

FmmisFileBuilder.cs: assembles FmmisTransactions into submission files
with ISA/IEA interchange envelope and separate GS/GE functional groups
for 837P and 837I. Extracts inner ST-SE segments from per-transaction
EDI, renumbers control numbers, enforces configurable per-file limit
(default 5000). File naming: FMMIS.{SubmitterId}.{yyyyMMdd_HHmmss}.dat.

Models/FmmisSubmissionResult.cs: add FmmisSubmissionFile (byte[] content,
claim IDs, transaction count) and rewrite FmmisSubmissionResult as a
tracking record with SubmissionId, Status enum (Pending/Accepted/
PartialAccept/Rejected), AcknowledgmentCode, and Errors list.

Models/FmmisValidationException.cs: rename Errors to ValidationErrors
to match spec convention.

Program.cs: register FmmisClaimTransformer and FmmisFileBuilder in the
claims-service DI container.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Scaffold encounter-submission-service microservice

New microservice tracking the 60-day AHCA encounter submission window
for FL Medicaid claims. Mirrors existing CHO microservice patterns
(appeals-service, eligibility-service).

Structure:
- Models/EncounterSubmission.cs: Cosmos DB document with TenantId
  partition key, ClaimId, adjudication date, submission deadline,
  status, batch tracking, 999 acknowledgment fields, retry count
- Models/EncounterSubmissionStatus.cs: enum (Pending, Batched,
  Submitted, Accepted, PartialAccept, Rejected, DeadlineWarning)
- Services/IEncounterSubmissionService.cs + impl: create, query,
  batch, submit, acknowledge lifecycle operations
- Controllers/EncounterSubmissionController.cs: REST API for
  operational visibility and manual intervention
- KafkaConsumers/AdjudicationCompletedConsumer.cs: listens to
  adjudication-completed topic, auto-creates submission records
  for FL Medicaid claims
- Workers/EncounterSubmissionWorker.cs: periodic deadline monitor
  (15-min interval) scanning for approaching deadlines
- Program.cs: standard CHO startup (Cosmos/Mongo, Kafka, health
  checks, tenant middleware, Swagger)
- Dockerfile, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json
- k8s/encounter-submission-service-deployment.yaml (ConfigMap +
  Deployment + Service)
- docker-compose.yml entry on port 5027

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Implement encounter submission business logic, worker, and HTTP clients

EncounterSubmissionServiceImpl — full MongoDB-backed implementation:
  1. CreateSubmissionRecordAsync: looks up TenantComplianceConfig via
     reference-data-service HTTP call to get EncounterSubmissionDays,
     calculates deadline, persists with Status=Pending
  2. GetPendingSubmissionsAsync: queries by tenant, ordered by deadline
     ascending, excludes Accepted and permanently Rejected (retries >= 3)
  3. BuildFmmisSubmissionBatchAsync: calls claims-service POST
     /api/claims/fmmis/batch with claim IDs, updates statuses to Batched
  4. ProcessAcknowledgmentAsync: parses 999 response (AK9/IK3/IK4
     segments), maps A/E/R to Accepted/PartialAccept/Rejected, increments
     RetryCount and populates LastError on rejections

EncounterSubmissionWorker — 4-hour interval (configurable):
  - Flags submissions within 7 days of deadline as DeadlineWarning
  - Publishes encounter-deadline-warning Kafka events via ProducerBuilder
  - Auto-batches submissions due within 48 hours per tenant
  - Writes batch files to Azure Blob fmmis-staging container

Program.cs — adds named HttpClient factories for ClaimsService and
ReferenceDataService (5-min handler lifetime, 10-30s timeout,
X-Tenant-ID header, Accept: application/json).

Also: Azure.Storage.Blobs 12.24.0 added to csproj, appsettings
updated with Worker:IntervalHours/UrgentBatchHours and AzureStorage
config, controller aligned with new interface signatures.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Add encounter submission API endpoints, Kafka events, and consumer gating

Controllers/EncounterSubmissionController.cs — rewrite with spec routes:
  GET  /api/encounters/{tenantId}/pending (paginated, deadline-ordered)
  GET  /api/encounters/{tenantId}/summary (counts by status for dashboard)
  GET  /api/encounters/{tenantId}/deadline-warnings (within N days)
  POST /api/encounters/{tenantId}/acknowledge (raw 999 body + batchId)
  POST /api/encounters/{tenantId}/retry/{submissionId} (manual retry)

Models/Events/EncounterEvents.cs — strongly-typed Kafka events:
  EncounterSubmissionCreatedEvent (submissionId, claimId, tenantId, deadline)
  EncounterDeadlineWarningEvent  (+ daysRemaining)
  EncounterSubmissionFailedEvent (+ errorMessage, retryCount)
  All with JsonPropertyName and UTC timestamp.

KafkaConsumers/AdjudicationCompletedConsumer.cs — three-gate filter:
  1. Status must be Approved, Paid, or PartiallyPaid
  2. Tenant must have StateCode configured
  3. Must be FL Medicaid (state=FL AND lob=Medicaid)
  Publishes EncounterSubmissionCreatedEvent on success.
  Added test constructor and ProcessMessageAsync overloads for unit testing.

Services — new methods: GetDeadlineWarningsAsync (tenant-scoped),
GetStatusSummaryAsync (counts by status), RetrySubmissionAsync
(reset Rejected→Pending with retry guard). GetPendingSubmissionsAsync
now supports page/pageSize pagination.

Worker updated to use EncounterDeadlineWarningEvent typed event.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Add MPIP provider qualification model and rate service

FL SMMC 3.0 Managed Medical Assistance Physician Incentive Program
implementation in provider-service.

Models/MpipProviderQualification.cs — Cosmos DB document:
  Id, TenantId (partition key), ProviderId, Npi, ProviderType
  (PrimaryCare/ObGyn/Specialist/Other), QualificationPeriod
  (FL fiscal year e.g. "2025-2026"), IsQualified, QualificationMethod
  (AutoQualified_Specialist/PerformanceBenchmark/NotQualified),
  EffectiveDate, ExpirationDate, EnhancedRateMultiplier (1.063 or 1.0),
  QualifiedByPlan, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt.

Services/MpipRateService.cs — IMpipRateService + implementation:
  - GetQualificationAsync: lookup by provider/tenant/period
  - GetEnhancedRateMultiplierAsync: core MPIP rate logic:
    * member age >= 21 → 1.0x (no enhancement)
    * Specialist + under 21 → 1.063x (auto-qualify)
    * PCP/OBGyn + IsQualified + under 21 → 1.063x
    * Otherwise → 1.0x
    Uses GetFiscalYearPeriod() to map service date to Oct 1-Sep 30 period.
  - UpsertQualificationAsync: create/update with auto-multiplier calc
  - GetQualifiedProvidersAsync: list qualified providers for a period
  MongoDB-backed with structured logging.

Program.cs: register IMpipRateService as scoped service.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Add MPIP controller, claims-service rate integration, and unit tests

Part A — provider-service/Controllers/MpipController.cs:
  GET  /api/mpip/{tenantId}/providers (list qualifications by period)
  GET  /api/mpip/{tenantId}/providers/{providerId} (single qualification)
  PUT  /api/mpip/{tenantId}/providers/{providerId} (upsert, admin)
  POST /api/mpip/{tenantId}/bulk-import (AHCA Oct 1 list import)
  GET  /api/mpip/{tenantId}/rate-check (pre-claim multiplier check
       with providerId, serviceDate, memberAge query params)

Part B — claims-service MPIP allowed amount integration:
  - ClaimLine.MpipMultiplierApplied (decimal?) added to record
    which multiplier was used during adjudication
  - MpipRateClient: calls provider-service GET /api/mpip/.../rate-check
    via named HttpClient with X-Tenant-ID header, graceful fallback to 1.0x
  - MpipAdjudicationEnhancer: checks TenantComplianceConfig.MpipEnabled
    via reference-data-service, then applies multiplier to each line's
    AllowedAmount/PaidAmount, recalculates claim-level totals, logs
    structured enhancement details (provider, age, multiplier, amounts)
  - ClaimsController.UpdateAdjudication: calls enhancer after base
    adjudication is set, before status determination, only for Medicaid LOB
  - Program.cs: registers ProviderService + ReferenceDataService named
    HttpClients (10s timeout, 5-min handler lifetime), IMpipRateClient,
    IMpipAdjudicationEnhancer as scoped services

Part C — tests/CloudHealthOffice.ProviderService.Tests:
  MpipRateServiceTests (xunit + Mongo2Go) covering all branches:
  - Specialist + age 19 → 1.063x (auto-qualify)
  - Specialist + age 21 → 1.0x (at boundary)
  - Specialist + age 22 → 1.0x (over)
  - PCP qualified + age 19 → 1.063x
  - PCP not qualified + age 19 → 1.0x
  - OB/GYN qualified + under 21 → 1.063x
  - Other type → 1.0x
  - No qualification record → 1.0x
  - Fiscal year: Oct 1 → new period, Sep 30 → prior period (6 Theory cases)
  - Upsert round-trip + GetQualifiedProviders filter

* Add FL AHCA full workflow E2E integration test

tests/CloudHealthOffice.FlAhca.E2E/ — validates the complete FL Medicaid
compliance workflow across all four units in a single test method:

FlAhcaFixture: docker-compose fixture waiting for claims-service (:5001),
benefit-plan-service (:5002), provider-service (:5004),
reference-data-service (:5011), encounter-submission-service (:5027).

FL_Medicaid_Claim_EncounterSubmission_E2E test steps:

Setup: seeds TenantComplianceConfig (MpipEnabled=true, StateCode=FL,
60-day encounter window, FMMIS credentials) and MPIP specialist
qualification for the current fiscal year.

Step 1 — FMMIS 837P: builds claim for Member A (age 19), calls
FmmisClaimTransformer.BuildEdi (pure function), asserts:
  - 2000B subscriber = member (HL*2*1*22*0, no 2000C dependent)
  - NM109 = Medicaid ID via MI qualifier
  - REF*1D = FL Medicaid Provider Number
  - ISA08 = FMMIS (padded to 15)
  - BHT02 = 18 (encounter purpose code)

Step 2 — MPIP adjudication: calls provider-service rate-check,
asserts multiplier = 1.063 for specialist + age 19. Submits
adjudication, verifies ClaimLine.MpipMultiplierApplied = 1.063.

Step 3 — Encounter submission: queries encounter-submission-service
pending list, verifies auto-created record with Status=Pending and
SubmissionDeadline = ClaimAdjudicatedAt + 60 days.

Step 4 — FMMIS batch file: builds batch via FmmisFileBuilder,
asserts filename FMMIS.{SubmitterId}.{timestamp}.dat,
TransactionCount=1, ISA/GS/ST/SE/GE/IEA structure.

Step 5 — 999 acknowledgment: posts AK9*A accepted 999 to
encounter-submission-service acknowledge endpoint.

Step 6 — Age >= 21: rate-check for Member B (age 22) returns
multiplier 1.0, enhancedRateApplies=false. Submits and adjudicates
claim, verifies no MPIP enhancement.

Step 7 — Deadline warning: queries deadline-warnings and summary
endpoints, verifies response structure with status counts.

Mirrors existing E2E patterns: DockerComposeFixture, ICollectionFixture,
anonymous request objects, JsonElement response parsing, permissive
status assertions, X-Tenant-ID header propagation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012Uk21fWEsQMb6mFNN6Lab6

* Address code review findings: security, tenant isolation, correctness

CodeQL log-injection fixes (19 findings):
  - MpipRateService: add Sanitize() helper, wrap all user-provided
    string values (providerId, tenantId, qualificationPeriod) in log
    statements across all methods
  - EncounterSubmissionService: add SanitizeForLog(), apply to all
    log calls with claimId, tenantId, batchId, submissionId, errorMessage
  - MpipController: sanitize qualification.ProviderId in bulk import log

Copilot reviewer fixes (15 findings):
  - CalculateMemberAge: return int? instead of 0 to prevent false MPIP
    triggering; callers skip enhancement when age is null
  - ProcessAcknowledgmentAsync: add tenantId parameter and include in
    MongoDB filter to prevent cross-tenant status changes
  - Batch update filter: add TenantId constraint for tenant isolation
  - BuildFmmisSubmissionBatch: replace non-existent POST /api/claims/
    fmmis/batch with per-claim GET /api/claims/{id} fetches
  - encounter-submission-service Program.cs: remove broken Cosmos DB
    fallback (service requires IMongoDatabase); require MongoDB config
  - k8s ConfigMap: fix Worker__IntervalMinutes -> Worker__IntervalHours,
    remove Cosmos env vars from deployment since service uses MongoDB
  - reference-data-service Program.cs: replace CosmosSystemTextJsonSerializer
    (missing namespace) with CosmosSerializationOptions
  - fl-ahca-config.json -> fl-ahca-config.jsonc (comments invalid in JSON)

commit.message:
fix: register WIF federated credential for platform service connection

When an ADO WIF service connection is created in 'manual' mode (providing an
existing SP instead of letting ADO create one), ADO does NOT automatically
add a federated identity credential to the Azure app registration. Without
the credential, Azure rejects the OIDC token when ADO tries to authenticate
for KV-linked variable group creation/refresh — 401 Unauthorized.

Changes:
- Add data.azuread_application.sp_crossplane to resolve the app object ID
  from the current client_id (no new variable needed)
- Add azuread_application_federated_identity_credential.platform_sc using
  the issuer/subject emitted by azuredevops_serviceendpoint_azurerm.main
- Add the federated credential to variable group depends_on so the credential
  is registered before ADO validates the KV connection
- Add mock_data azuread_application block to all 5 test files

commit.message:
feat: complete 3-step auth — B2C → JWT exchange → OAuth SSO

The full NES auth flow is:
1. B2C headless login (PKCE) → id_token
2. GET /rest/auth/jwt?id_token=... → 302 redirect with SSO UUID token
   (requires browser User-Agent header; CloudFront WAF blocks non-browsers)
3. POST /rest/oauth/token with logintype=sso, usertoken=UUID → NES API token

Key discovery: /rest/auth/jwt creates a server-side session and returns
a short UUID in the redirect URL (/#/ssohome/<uuid>), NOT the raw
id_token. That UUID is what the Angular app passes to getSSOToken().

Tested locally: full chain succeeds through to customer API returning data.

commit.message:
Avoid `const_cast` in methods on `PEDecoder` (#126672)

## Description

`PEDecoder` uses `const_cast<PEDecoder *>(this)->` in 8 places across
`const` methods to lazily cache validation flags and header pointers.
This is the textbook use case for `mutable`.

Mark the four cache fields as `mutable` and remove all `const_cast`
usages:

- **`src/coreclr/inc/pedecoder.h`** — Add `mutable` to `m_flags`,
`m_pNTHeaders`, `m_pCorHeader`, `m_pReadyToRunHeader`
- **`src/coreclr/utilcode/pedecoder.cpp`** — Remove 7 `const_cast` sites
(`HasNTHeaders`, `CheckNTHeaders`, `CheckCorHeader`, `CheckILOnly` ×2,
`FindReadyToRunHeader` ×2)
- **`src/coreclr/inc/pedecoder.inl`** — Remove 1 `const_cast` site
(`GetCorHeader`)

No layout or behavioral change — `mutable` only affects
const-correctness at compile time.

commit.message:
Iterations push to existing PR branch instead of creating new PRs

When a reviewer submits further feedback from a PR comparison page,
the issue body now includes PR context (branch name, PR number). The
feedback service detects this and:
- Checks out the existing PR branch instead of creating a new one
- Pushes a new commit with the iteration fixes
- Comments on the existing PR about the new commit
- Publishes an updated gist comparison (with PR context for further
  iterations)
- Does NOT create a duplicate PR

This keeps the full review history in a single PR. The reviewer can
wait to merge until all iterations are satisfactory.
